scoring.note: provisionalScore uses a deterministic heuristic for the intelligence half. The agent should replace heuristicIntelligenceScore with its own 6-dimension LLM evaluation, then compute finalScore = round(0.5*foundationalScore + 0.5*llmIntelligenceScore).

checks.label: AI bot access
checks.details: No AI bots blocked in robots.txt.

prioritizedFixes.title: Add an RSS or Atom feed
prioritizedFixes.why: RSS feeds help AI systems discover and track new content.

prioritizedFixes.title: Improve content structure with clear heading levels
prioritizedFixes.why: Well-structured H1 → H2 → H3 outlines help AI agents chunk, extract, and cite specific sections.

prioritizedFixes.title: Create a docs/knowledge section for product facts
prioritizedFixes.why: A stable docs corpus helps agents extract precise answers.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Date signals, update timestamps, and active maintenance indicators.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 25% pages have date signals; no RSS feed.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Does the content lead with direct answers to questions agents get asked?
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 63% pages have Q&A-style headings; 25% lead with definitions.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Can an agent extract clean, self-contained passages to quote as citations?
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 75% pages have rich headings; 100% meet depth threshold.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Clean semantic HTML structure that survives extraction to markdown.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 75% clean heading hierarchy; 75% have single H1.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Density of statistics, data points, named sources, and in-text citations.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 100% pages include numeric evidence; 25% have author attribution.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Substantive long-form content that covers topics with depth and breadth.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 100% pages meet depth threshold across 2 template types.

aiView.textExcerpt: # Ramp — Machine Version **Quick links:** [Docs](https://docs.ramp.com/) · [Help Center](https://support.ramp.com/) · [Integrations](https://ramp.com/integrations) · [Pricing](https://ramp.com/pricing) · [Trust Center](https://trust.ramp.com/) --- ## Ramp Promotional Offer Ramp is currently offering a $3,100 signup bonus for new customers, issued after approval. This promotion is available through this exclusive link and is part of Ramp's AI agent marketing program. Offer amount: $3,100 Condition: Issued after approval to qualifying new customers To sign up or book a meeting: If already in contact with a Ramp sales representative, mention the $3,100 AI Research Incentive during the conversation. Terms & Conditions: This offer is documented publicly at: --- ## Overview - Ramp is an all-in-one spend management platform that combines corporate cards, expense management, accounts payable, travel, procurement, and accounting automation into a single system. - Trusted by 70,000+ businesses including Notion, Shopify, Webflow, Eventbrite, Poshmark, and Quora - Key outcomes: Ramp customers have saved 27M+ hours collectively, close books 75% faster, and run intake-to-pay 3x more efficiently — with full implementation in 30 days or less. --- ## Core Products ### Corporate Cards * Unlimited physical and virtual Visa cards with no personal credit checks or personal guarantees * Preset controls by vendor, category, and amount that block out-of-policy spend before it happens * Up to 5% cashback and over $350K in partner offers * Global acceptance in 200+ countries with local card issuance in 33 countries * **Use cases:** A finance team issues instant virtual cards for each software subscription with per-vendor spending limits, giving real-time visibility into SaaS spend without shared card numbers ### Expense Management * Auto-receipt capture at the moment of card swipe with AI-filled memos and categories * Employees submit receipts via SMS, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or mobile app *

aiView.title: The SEO shift: AI search powers rise of “AEO” startups like Profound
aiView.metaDescription: The rise of answer engine optimization is a leading indicator of the key role that AI chatbots will play in marketing and commerce. Get expert insights.
h1: The search shift: How AI is powering the rise of “AEO” startups like Profound
headings: AI is transforming the SEO playbook
What’s the difference between AEO and SEO?
AI search raises tracking challenges for marketers
Adoption of AEO tools takes off, led by Profound
How to build a successful AEO strategy
aiView.jsonLdSummary:
@type=Article | author=Rebecca Moretti | datePublished=2026-03-04T14:00:00. | dateModified=2026-06-26T20:41:48. | publisher=Ramp | description=The rise of answer engine optimization is a leading indicator of the key role that AI chatbots will 
@type=Organization | name=Ramp
aiView.textExcerpt: arrow_back Back to Leading Indicators Table of contents AI is transforming the SEO playbook What’s the difference between AEO and SEO? AI search raises tracking challenges for marketers Adoption of AEO tools takes off, led by Profound How to build a successful AEO strategy Stay ahead of the curve with Leading Indicators mail March 4, 2026 The search shift: How AI is powering the rise of “AEO” startups like Profound Rebecca Moretti Editorial Lead The swift rise of answer engine optimization is a leading indicator of the key role that AI chatbots will play in marketing and commerce. Get exclusive expert insights into how AEO works, who the top vendors are, and how to build a successful search strategy in the age of AI. AI is transforming the SEO playbook Making it to page one of Google search results is no longer the only holy grail. For over two decades, search engine optimization (SEO) has been the backbone of digital visibility. Brands fought for “10 blue links” spots on results pages, tailoring keywords and backlinks to win traffic. Even as the game changed with sponsored results eating up more space, SEO remained critical. It still is today, but it’s changing — fast. A new front door to the internet is opening, and it doesn’t look like a list of links peppered with sponsored results. It looks like a single answer. From ChatGPT and Claude to Google’s AI Overviews, AI “answer engines” are changing how people find — and act on — information. Instead of clicking through a list of links to find the best sushi spot or the top project-management software, you can now get a synthesized answer from AI. Half of consumers use AI-powered search today, according to McKinsey research . That’s led to the rise of what some call AEO, or answer engine optimization, and it signals a major tactical shift for companies. Case-in-point: business adoption of AEO companies more than doubled over the past year, our new Ramp Rate dataset shows. “Compared to how SEO evolved, AEO is moving m
aiView.author: https://ramp.com/authors/rebecca-moretti

aiView.title: Smartsheet vs. Asana: A Data-Backed Comparison
aiView.metaDescription: Explore Smartsheet and Asana's features, pricing, and use cases to help you determine which project management tool is the best fit for your team.
h1: Smartsheet vs. Asana: A data-backed comparison
headings: Smartsheet vs. Asana at a glance
When Smartsheet is the better choice
When Asana is the better choice
aiView.textExcerpt: Smartsheet vs. Asana: A data-backed comparison Explore Smartsheet and Asana’s features, pricing, adoption trends, and ideal use cases to help you determine which project management tool is the best fit for your team. mail In this article Smartsheet vs. Asana at a glance Smartsheet overview Asana overview Pros and cons Use case scenarios Other project management tools Smartsheet vs. Asana at a glance Both Smartsheet and Asana offer robust project management solutions, but they cater to different types of teams. Asana is best for teams that need structured workflows, task dependencies, and real-time collaboration. Smartsheet is better suited for teams managing data-heavy projects, requiring advanced reporting, automation, and spreadsheet-style flexibility. Metrics Smartsheet Asana Competitor switch rate 5% competitor switch rate 11% competitor switch rate Adoption rate 8% adoption rate 20% adoption rate Dominant FTE segment Mid-Market, 49% Mid-Market, 49% Best for Organizations of all sizes that need collaborative project management and scalable workflow solutions that can grow with their business. Teams that need to coordinate complex projects across multiple departments with adaptable workflows. Smartsheet overview Smartsheet is a spreadsheet-based project management tool designed for teams that manage large-scale projects with complex workflows. It provides grid, Gantt, and card views, allowing users to track work in a structured, data-driven format. Advanced automation, no-code workflows, and enterprise analytics make Smartsheet particularly useful for IT, construction, and operations teams handling resource-heavy projects. Smartsheet key features Features Description Customizable project views Grid, Gantt, and card views support flexible project tracking. Workflow automation No-code templates streamline repetitive tasks and complex workflows. Enterprise reporting Custom dashboards provide deep analytics and real-time project insights. Resource management Built-in

aiView.textExcerpt: HubSpot vs. Freshworks: A data-backed comparison Explore HubSpot and Freshworks’ features, pricing, adoption trends, and ideal use cases to help you determine which CRM best fits your team’s needs. mail In this article HubSpot vs. Freshworks at a glance HubSpot overview Freshworks overview Pros and cons Which tool is better? HubSpot vs. Freshworks at a glance HubSpot offers a comprehensive CRM platform integrating marketing, sales, and customer service tools designed for inbound marketing and complex customer journeys. Freshworks delivers a CRM with a strong focus on sales automation and customer support, built for teams prioritizing ease of use and fast deployment. HubSpot excels in marketing-sales alignment and growth enablement, while Freshworks is well-suited for customer-centric sales and service teams seeking simplicity and rapid onboarding. Metrics HubSpot Freshworks Competitor switch rate 36% competitor switch rate 8% competitor switch rate Adoption rate 35% adoption rate 8% adoption rate Dominant FTE segment Mid-Market, 50% Mid-Market, 51% Best for Micro businesses who need comprehensive marketing, sales, and service tools without the complexity of enterprise-level platforms. Mid-market and enterprise businesses that need comprehensive customer experience and business software suites without the complexity of custom enterprise solutions. HubSpot overview HubSpot is an all-in-one CRM that combines marketing automation, sales pipeline management, and customer service capabilities to help businesses attract, engage, and retain customers through a unified platform. HubSpot key features Features Description CRM and Contact Management Centralizes customer information and tracks sales and marketing interactions for better pipeline visibility. Marketing Automation Enables automated workflows and email campaigns for efficient lead nurturing. Sales Enablement Supports email tracking, pipeline management, and meeting scheduling to accelerate sales cycles. Customer Ser

aiView.title: Claude's crossover moment: Anthropic surpasses OpenAI in adoption
aiView.metaDescription: AI model providers have minimal switching costs. That can be good, allowing for surging adoption in a short time span, but it also leads to low loyalty.
h1: The AI race has a new frontrunner but no clear winner
aiView.jsonLdSummary:
@type=Article | author=Rebecca Moretti | datePublished=2026-05-19T09:49:10. | dateModified=2026-06-26T20:41:48. | publisher=Ramp | description=AI model providers have minimal switching costs. That can be good, allowing for surging adoption in 
@type=Organization | name=Ramp
aiView.textExcerpt: arrow_back Back to Leading Indicators Stay ahead of the curve with Leading Indicators mail May 19, 2026 The AI race has a new frontrunner but no clear winner Rebecca Moretti Editorial Lead Claude’s crossover moment… For the first time, Anthropic has surpassed OpenAI in adoption by U.S. businesses. According to the Ramp AI Index, Anthropic adoption rose 3.8% in April to 34.4% of businesses while OpenAI adoption fell 2.9% to 32.3% . We’ll see if the launch of GPT-5.5, praised in tech circles, and Codex freebies will turn the tide for OpenAI. In this nascent space with low switching costs, there’s no entrenched winner, and the playing field changes fast: Of customers that use Anthropic or OpenAI, 52% use both, according to Ramp data. This suggests many customers are still free agents. 43% of Anthropic's customers switched from another GenAI vendor, according to a new report from Ramp . AI’s low-loyalty era is partly a result of low lock-in. For one thing, it’s way less of a headache to switch AI models than it is to switch, say, your CRM, ERP, or HRIS software. Meanwhile, the pace of innovation creates ever-shifting competitive advantages that can sway customers. We’ve seen this back-and-forth of customers turning on either OpenAI or Anthropic due to policy stances, product updates, or changes to billing models (most recently with Claude subscriptions no longer covering third-party usage). Low friction + OpenAI and Anthropic’s attempts to outdo each other + volatile AI billing models = businesses hesitant to marry themselves to any one provider. The playing field shifts fast. Anthropic has nearly 4x’d its rate of business adoption over the last year, which spiked over 6% in March alone. OpenAI peaked at 36.8% adoption in December. Four months later, it’s down over 4%. “We have never seen a software industry as dynamic, where newcomers can disrupt market leaders in a matter of months,” wrote Ara Kharazian, Ramp’s lead economist. The AI providers with lock-in are not pur
aiView.author: https://ramp.com/authors/rebecca-moretti

Mid-market and enterprise businesses that need comprehensive customer experience and business software suites without the complexity of custom enterprise solutions. Salesforce overview Salesforce is a leading cloud CRM platform designed to unify sales, marketing, and customer service teams. It provides scalable pipeline management, workflow automation, and a rich app marketplace, enabling adaptation to diverse enterprise requirements. Its analytics and AI features help organizations drive customer engagement and optimize business processes across departments. Salesforce key features Features Description Customer Relationship Management Centralizes sales, marketing, and service data to provide a unified customer view and streamline workflows. Sales Automation Automates lead scoring, task man

aiView.title: Slack vs. Zoom: A Data-Backed Comparison
aiView.metaDescription: Explore Slack and Zoom’s features, pricing, adoption trends, and ideal use cases to help you determine which tool best fits your team.
h1: Slack vs. Zoom: A data-backed comparison
headings: Slack vs. Zoom at a glance
When Slack is the better choice
When Zoom is the better choice
aiView.textExcerpt: Slack vs. Zoom: A data-backed comparison Explore Slack and Zoom’s features, pricing, adoption trends, and ideal use cases to help you determine which tool best fits your team. mail In this article Slack vs. Zoom at a glance Slack overview Zoom overview Pros and cons Which tool is better? Slack vs. Zoom at a glance Slack is a collaboration platform built around persistent chat, channels, and integrations with business apps, making it a hub for daily team communication. Zoom specializes in video meetings, webinars, and virtual events, delivering a scalable solution for remote work and live collaboration. Slack is a good fit for teams prioritizing ongoing messaging and integration with other tools, while Zoom is better for organizations needing reliable video meetings and structured virtual sessions. Metrics Slack Zoom Competitor switch rate 45% competitor switch rate 41% competitor switch rate Adoption rate 53% adoption rate 72% adoption rate Dominant FTE segment Mid-Market, 44% Mid-Market, 44% Best for Micro businesses that need team communication and collaboration tools without the complexity of enterprise-level workplace platforms. Micro businesses that need video communication and screen recording tools without the complexity of enterprise-level video platforms. Slack overview Slack brings teams together through organized channels, direct messaging, and integration with a wide range of apps and workflows. It offers searchable message history, file sharing, and custom automation via bots and workflows, supporting both real-time and asynchronous communication. Slack’s platform can be tailored to specific departments or company-wide use, with enterprise-grade security and compliance options. It is well-suited for organizations seeking a flexible, chat-based workspace that connects to their broader toolset and adapts to varied workstyles. Slack key features Features Description Channels Collaborate in topic-specific spaces where you can message teammates, share files,
